MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-08-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Mark A. Love of SELMAN BREITMAN (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / moving party Foster Lumber Yard, Inc.
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Gary Kase and Irene Kase

RULING:
DEFENDANT FOSTER LUMBER YARD, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Foster Lumber Yard, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. As to summary judgment, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Kase was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to summary adjudication, adjudication of issue 1 (negligence-substantial factor causation) is denied because defendant failed to properly make an issue of substantial factor causation or failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Kase's exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his risk of developing his asbestos-related illness. Adjudication of issues 2 & 3 (negligence-reasonable care; negligence-foreseeability) is denied because Dr. Cohen's declaration creates a triable issue whether defendant failed to exercise reasonable care and whether Mr. Kase's injuries were foreseeable. Adjudication of issues 4 and 5 (strict liability) is denied on the same grounds as the denial of summary judgment. Adjudication of issue 6 (loss of consortium) is denied because defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that all of plaintiffs' underlying claims must fail. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara A. Lohman of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
